stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML technologies including transformers, safetensors, and text-generation.
This indicates active community interest and benchmarking activity around a significant open-source LLM.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the core area of large language models (LLMs) and text generation.
The associated tags indicate relevance to transformers, safetensors, and the Llama family of models.

stories.title: MetaBackdoor: Exploiting Positional Encoding as a Backdoor Attack Surface in LLMs
stories.author: Ahmed Salem
bullet_points: Introduces 'MetaBackdoor,' a novel class of backdoor attack against Large Language Models (LLMs).
Challenges the assumption that LLM backdoors must rely on explicit content-based triggers.
Exploits positional encoding within LLMs as a covert and powerful attack surface.

stories.title: Talk is (Not) Cheap: A Taxonomy and Benchmark Coverage Audit for LLM Attacks
stories.author: Alexey A. Shvets
bullet_points: Introduces a novel, reusable framework to audit the collective coverage of LLM attack benchmarks.
The framework uses a 4x6 Target x Technique matrix, grounded in the STRIDE threat model, derived from 932 arXiv security studies.
Its primary value is enabling 'benchmark-external validation' to assess the overall threat surface coverage of existing LLM security research.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face.
The model is categorized for text-generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML technologies like transformers and PyTorch.
The pipeline indicates the model is being used for text generation tasks.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for enhanced performance.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon devices, optimizing AI workloads on Apple hardware.
The feature is currently available in a preview state, indicating ongoing development and optimization.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs entirely on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing AI assistance while maintaining data privacy through local execution.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to simplify the setup and execution of coding tools.
It allows running various coding models (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) using both local and cloud infrastructure.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification tasks.
The models are available in two sizes (20B and 120B) and are permissively licensed under Apache 2.0.
This partnership with OpenAI and ROOST aims to bring advanced safety tools to the open-source community.

stories.title: A revolutionary cancer treatment could transform autoimmune disease
stories.author: Amber Dance, Knowable Magazine
bullet_points: CAR T cell therapy is being investigated for treating autoimmune diseases.
The therapy aims to 'reset' the immune system, a significant biological intervention.
The research represents a potential paradigm shift in treating complex immune disorders.

stories.title: The US is betting on AI to catch insider trading in prediction markets
stories.author: Kate Knibbs, wired.com
bullet_points: The CFTC is actively utilizing AI to combat insider trading.
The focus is on leveraging AI within prediction markets for regulatory oversight.
This signals a significant shift in how financial regulators are adopting advanced AI technologies.

stories.title: Anthropic’s $1.5B copyright settlement is getting messy as judge delays approval
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: Anthropic is involved in a significant copyright settlement valued at $1.5 billion.
The approval of this historic settlement is facing delays from the presiding judge.
Legal disputes have arisen, with accusations that lawyers attempted to rush the settlement to secure substantial fees.

stories.title: Forgetting That Sticks: Quantization-Permanent Unlearning via Circuit Attribution
stories.author: Vinay Kumar Sankarapu
bullet_points: Addresses a critical gap in AI safety: the failure of standard unlearning evaluations to account for post-training quantization.
Demonstrates a systematic dual failure where gradient-based forgetting methods are reversed by 4-bit quantization, while quantization-robust methods fail to forget effectively.
Proposes a novel approach, 'Quantization-Permanent Unlearning via Circuit Attribution,' to address this compression-induced forgetting.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It facilitates the use of proprietary models (like Claude) alongside open-source models.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a working directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: ElliQ is a surprisingly helpful companion robot for older adults
stories.author: Sheena Vasani
bullet_points: ElliQ is a companion robot designed to assist older adults, particularly those with conditions like Parkinson's.
The robot aims to help users rebalance their lives by encouraging crucial activities such as exercise, socialization, and hobbies.
The story highlights the practical application of robotics in addressing age-related health and cognitive decline.

stories.title: Snap, YouTube, and TikTok settle suit over harm to students
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: Snap, YouTube, and TikTok settled a lawsuit regarding the impact of social media on students.
The suit, brought by a Kentucky school district, alleges social media addiction has caused financial strain and disrupted learning.
The case highlights growing concerns over the mental health crisis linked to social media use in educational settings.

stories.title: Three's a party: US, China, and now Russia are on the prowl in GEO
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: US, China, and Russia are actively increasing their surveillance and presence in Geosynchronous Orbit (GEO).
The article suggests that satellites are not as stealthy as previously assumed, as they are often visible against the blackness of space.
The geopolitical tension is escalating in space, moving beyond traditional silent operations.

stories.title: EntityBench: Towards Entity-Consistent Long-Range Multi-Shot Video Generation
stories.author: Vicente Ordonez
bullet_points: Introduces EntityBench, a new benchmark for evaluating long-range, multi-shot video generation.
Addresses the key challenge in video generation: maintaining entity consistency (characters, objects, locations) across long sequences.
The benchmark is derived from real narrative media, offering a more rigorous evaluation standard than previous methods.

stories.title: ATLAS: Agentic or Latent Visual Reasoning? One Word is Enough for Both
stories.author: Pheng-Ann Heng
bullet_points: The paper discusses two approaches to visual reasoning: agentic (using code/tools) and latent (using hidden embeddings).
Directly generating images during reasoning is noted as computationally expensive and complex.
The research compares the trade-offs, such as context-switching latency in agentic methods versus the complexity of latent representations.

stories.title: FutureSim: Replaying World Events to Evaluate Adaptive Agents
stories.author: Jonas Geiping
bullet_points: Introduces FutureSim, a new simulation framework designed to test AI agents in dynamic, real-world environments.
The core innovation is replaying actual world events chronologically, allowing agents to interact with historical data as it unfolded.
This setup enables agents to forecast future events beyond their training data cutoff while maintaining chronological context.

stories.title: VGGT-Edit: Feed-forward Native 3D Scene Editing with Residual Field Prediction
stories.author: Wentao Zhang
bullet_points: VGGT-Edit introduces a new method for native 3D scene editing using a feed-forward architecture.
It addresses the limitation of current 3D reconstruction models, which struggle with dynamic human instructions.
The approach moves beyond traditional 2D-lifting strategies for scene modification.

stories.title: Quantitative Video World Model Evaluation for Geometric-Consistency
stories.author: Xueyan Zou
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of quantitatively evaluating the 3D physical plausibility of generative video models.
Introduces PDI-Bench (Perspective Distortion Index), a new quantitative framework for auditing geometric coherence.
Moves beyond subjective human judgment or learned graders for video quality assessment.

stories.title: Eradicating Negative Transfer in Multi-Physics Foundation Models via Sparse Mixture-of-Experts Routing
stories.author: Arastu Sharma
bullet_points: Addresses 'negative transfer' in scaling Scientific Machine Learning (SciML) foundation models.
The problem arises from co-training disparate Partial Differential Equation (PDE) regimes, causing gradient conflict in dense models.
Proposes a solution using Sparse Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) routing to manage incompatible demands from different physical simulations.

stories.title: OpenDeepThink: Parallel Reasoning via Bradley--Terry Aggregation
stories.author: Jingbo Shang
bullet_points: Introduces OpenDeepThink, a new framework for improving LLM reasoning by scaling compute breadth.
Addresses the 'selection bottleneck' in parallel reasoning by avoiding noisy, pointwise LLM judging.
Utilizes pairwise Bradley-Terry aggregation to select the best reasoning candidate from a population.

stories.title: Evidential Reasoning Advances Interpretable Real-World Disease Screening
stories.author: Jing Qin
bullet_points: Introduces EviScreen, a new evidential reasoning framework for disease screening.
Addresses limitations in current medical image models, specifically poor interpretability and lack of historical context.
Leverages region-level evidence from historical cases to provide transparent reasoning pathways in clinical screening.

stories.title: Text Knows What, Tables Know When: Clinical Timeline Reconstruction via Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment
stories.author: Jeremy C. Weiss
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of reconstructing precise clinical timelines from patient data.
It combines unstructured clinical narratives (rich context) with structured EHR data (temporal precision).
The proposed method uses Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment to bridge the gap between narrative and structured timing.

stories.title: Position: Behavioural Assurance Cannot Verify the Safety Claims Governance Now Demands
stories.author: Vinay Kumar Sankarapu
bullet_points: Behavioral assurance methods are insufficient to verify the stringent safety claims required by current AI governance frameworks.
Governance demands verifiable evidence for properties like absence of hidden objectives and bounded catastrophic capability.
Current assurance techniques (behavioral evaluations, red-teaming) are limited to observable model outputs, creating an epistemological gap.

stories.title: FutureSim: Replaying World Events to Evaluate Adaptive Agents
stories.author: Jonas Geiping
bullet_points: Introduces FutureSim, a new simulation framework for testing AI agents in dynamic environments.
The system replays real-world events chronologically, allowing agents to interact with historical data streams.
Aims to evaluate an agent's ability to adapt and forecast events beyond its initial knowledge cutoff.

stories.title: Is Grep All You Need? How Agent Harnesses Reshape Agentic Search
stories.author: Vamse Kumar Subbiah
bullet_points: The paper investigates how different retrieval strategies impact agentic search systems powered by LLMs.
It addresses a gap in current literature by systematically comparing retrieval choice against agent architecture and tool-calling paradigms.
The research focuses on complex agentic workflows where LLMs autonomously retrieve information and use tools to complete tasks.

stories.title: Text Knows What, Tables Know When: Clinical Timeline Reconstruction via Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment
stories.author: Jeremy C. Weiss
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of reconstructing precise clinical timelines from patient data.
It combines unstructured clinical narratives (rich context) with structured EHR data (precise timing).
The proposed method uses Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment to bridge the gap between narrative richness and temporal accuracy.

stories.title: MeMo: Memory as a Model
stories.author: Armando Solar-Lezama
bullet_points: Introduces MeMo (Memory as a Model), a framework to update LLMs without retraining core parameters.
Addresses the limitation of LLMs being 'frozen' after pretraining, which hinders real-world applicability.
Encodes new, domain-specific knowledge into a dedicated memory model for efficient knowledge integration.

stories.title: Self-Distilled Agentic Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: Yongliang Shen
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limitation of coarse, trajectory-level reward signals in Reinforcement Learning (RL) for LLM agents.
It introduces On-Policy Self-Distillation (OPSD) to provide dense, token-level guidance using a teacher branch with privileged context.
A key challenge identified is the instability of transferring OPSD to multi-turn agents due to compounding supervision issues.

stories.title: MemEye: A Visual-Centric Evaluation Framework for Multimodal Agent Memory
stories.author: Ruixiang Tang
bullet_points: Introduces MemEye, a new evaluation framework for multimodal agent memory.
Addresses a gap where existing evaluations often rely only on textual traces, ignoring fine-grained visual evidence.
Focuses on testing agents' ability to preserve visual evidence necessary for complex, state-changing reasoning.

stories.title: Show HN: Semble – Code search for agents that uses 98% fewer tokens than grep
stories.author: Bibabomas
bullet_points: Semble is an open-sourced tool designed to improve code search within large codebases for AI agents.
It addresses the high token consumption issue when agents fall back to traditional methods like grep.
The tool claims to use 98% fewer tokens than grep while maintaining retrieval quality.

stories.title: The Delusion of Infinite Compute: Running Gemma 4 on an i5 CPU
stories.author: kaushikcoderpy
bullet_points: The article explores running the Gemma 4 model on a standard i5 CPU, challenging the necessity of high-end GPUs.
It addresses the concept of 'infinite compute' in the context of accessible local LLM deployment.
The content is framed as a submission for a specific Gemma 4 Challenge, indicating a practical, hands-on focus.

stories.title: How AI Agents Will Reshape E-Commerce Architecture — And What Developers Need to Build Now
stories.author: shakti_mishra_308e9f36b5d
bullet_points: AI agents are predicted to fundamentally change e-commerce interaction models.
The future customer experience involves delegation to AI, rather than traditional clicking.
Developers are advised on necessary skills and architectural changes to prepare for this shift.

stories.title: Agent Benchmark Scores Are Measuring the Harness, Not the Model | Focused Labs
stories.author: austinbv
bullet_points: Agent benchmarks are criticized for measuring the surrounding infrastructure rather than the core model capability.
The performance measured includes factors like the harness, runtime limits, and available tools.
The article suggests that observability and retry mechanisms significantly influence benchmark scores.
